items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Register for a Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

Can art inform the classic mind-body problem? While the relationship between mental activity and physical brain continues to baffle, can the existence and process of art provide insight?

Nathan A. Jacobs is a philosopher, artist, and filmmaker. He is a Visiting Scholar of Philosophy at the University of Kentucky.

items.title: Richard Dawkins - Evolution vs. Religion?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Follow us on Instagram for daily news, announcements, and exciting updates: https://shorturl.at/p2IhM

Two kinds of questions describe the relationship between evolution and religion. The first explores discrepancies, even contradictions, between the science of evolution and the beliefs of religion. The second examines how religion itself evolved.

Donate to Closer To Truth to help us keep our content free and without paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Richard Dawkins FRS FRSL is a British evolutionary biologist, zoologist, science communicator, and author, born in Africa. He is an emeritus fellow of New College, Oxford, and was Professor for Public Understanding of Science in the University of Oxford from 1995 to 2008.

items.title: Brian Leftow - What are Possible Worlds?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Listen to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts. Subscribe today: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

‘Possible worlds’ are all the ways in which a world can be. A ‘world’ is a complete state of cosmic affairs: all the infinite variations of innumerable parts and their myriad relationships. Possible world’s have become an indispensable philosophical technique for exploring modalities—issues of possibility and necessity that are essential for examining God.

Make a tax-deductible donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Brian Leftow is an American philosopher specializing in philosophy of religion, medieval philosophy, and metaphysics. He is the William P. Alston Professor for the Philosophy of Religion at Rutgers University.

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Listen to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts. Subscribe today: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

How do physicists and philosophers who believe in God harmonize the discoveries of cosmology with the doctrine of creation? How does cosmology enhance or erode their belief in God? If God exists, must the universe have had a beginning? Conversely, if the universe had a beginning, must God exist?

She received the B.A. from Creighton University in 1973, the Ph.D. from University of California, Berkeley in 1980, and the Th.D. from the Graduate Theological Union in 1987.

items.title: Eric Steinhart - Is Atheism a Belief System?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Shop T-shirts, hoodies, mugs, notebooks, and more in the Closer To Truth merch store: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Is atheism a belief system, a new ‘faith’ as it were, in the same way that atheists claim that theism is a belief system? Atheists reject this attack asserting that they are just using critical reasoning to expose irrational theism. What would make atheism is a new ‘faith’? Does atheism, like theism, have its own assumptions and ways of thinking?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Eric Charles Steinhart is Professor of Philosophy at William Paterson University. Among many articles and books, he has authored Your Digital Afterlives: Computational Theories of Life after Death and Believing in Dawkins: The New Spiritual Atheism.

items.title: Niels Gregersen - Did Animals Suffer in Evolution?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Sentient suffering is a hard problem for theists to solve. The ‘free will defense,’ that privileges and blames human choice, claims to cover moral evil. But what about natural evil, such as animals being killed or eaten alive continuously during the process of evolution before humans? Shouldn’t egregious and relentless animal suffering count against God?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Niels Henrik Gregersen is a professor in the faculty of theology at the University of Copenhagen. At Aarhus University he was Assistant Professor in Ethics and Philosophy of Religion, Associate Professor in Systematic Theology, and Research Professor in Theology & Science.

items.title: Tom McLeish - Are Science & Religion at War?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

What is it about science and theology that grips people so emotionally, as during a war, such that each is committed to his or her viewpoint (which often are sharply opposed to one another)? Each is fighting for its predominance, if not for its life: religion from being marginalized, science from being politically contained.

Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Thomas Charles Buckland McLeish FRS FRSC FInstP was a British theoretical physicist. His work is renowned for increasing understanding of the properties of soft matter. This is a matter that can be easily changed by stress – including liquids, foams and biological materials.

items.title: Kevin Timpe - Why is There Sin?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Follow us on X for daily news, updates, articles, and more: https://x.com/CloserToTruth

‘Sin’ is a term loaded with baggage. Different groups, mainly religious, use ‘sin’ to describe violations of the given order. Perpetrators of sin – ‘sinners’ – are punished in one form or another (in this life or the next). In the Christian tradition, where ‘sin’ plays a vital role, can we get an analytical description? What can we learn from the concept of sin?

Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Kevin Timpe currently holds the William H. Jellema Chair in Christian Philosophy at Calvin College. He's been a visiting faculty or scholar at Calvin Theological Seminary, Innsbruck University, Oxford University, and Peking University. He has written or edited ten books and published nearly 50 articles.

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:

Richard Granville Swinburne FBA is an English philosopher. He is an Emeritus Professor of Philosophy at the University of Oxford. Over the last 50 years, Swinburne has been a proponent of philosophical arguments for the existence of God.</body></html>

items.title: Peter Atkins - Arguments Against God?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Listen to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts. Subscribe today: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Theists must consider arguments against God. They should assess both the strong attacks of atheists and the counterattacks of scholarly believers who try to rebut non-believers. Belief in God is too important to be determined by default cultural circumstances. Arguments are a hedge against naive assumptions about reality.

Make a tax-deductible donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Peter William Atkins FRSC is an English chemist and a Fellow of Lincoln College at the University of Oxford. He retired in 2007. He is a prolific writer of popular chemistry textbooks, including Physical Chemistry, Inorganic Chemistry, and Molecular Quantum Mechanics. Atkins is also the author of a number of popular science books, including "Atkins' Molecules" and "Galileo's Finger: The Ten Great Ideas of Science and On Being".

items.title: Deepak Chopra - Must the Universe Contain Consciousness?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Do you enjoy our content? Make a donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making videos like this one: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Our universe seems fine-tuned for life, with the constants of physical laws having to be within tight boundaries. Does this mean that the universe has a goal of consciousness? Is there a directedness of the universe toward consciousness? Is consciousness entirely contingent or is it something special, even a ultimate object of universal development?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Deepak Chopra is an Indian-American author, new age guru, and alternative medicine advocate. A prominent figure in the New Age movement, his books and videos have made him one of the best-known and wealthiest figures in alternative medicine.

items.title: William Lane Craig - Is God Outside of Time?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Sign up for a Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

If God exists outside of time—not in time, but timeless and eternal—what would that mean about God’s nature? God would never ‘lose’ the past or anticipate the future. God would not need to know the future, because to a timeless God, there would be no future. Everything would be ever-present in one timeless moment. But how could a timeless God relate to human beings?

Support the show with a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring life's deepest questions: shorturl.at/OnyRq

William Lane Craig is an American analytic philosopher, Christian apologist, author, and Wesleyan theologian who upholds the view of Molinism and neo-Apollinarianism. He is a professor of philosophy at Houston Christian University and at the Talbot School of Theology of Biola University.

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Follow us on X for daily articles, updates, and more: https://x.com/CloserToTruth

What is the farthest extent of the physical world, not only cosmologically across the universe but also conceptually across laws of nature? Can there be radical new discoveries in the 21st century as relativity and quantum mechanics were in the 20th century? Some speculate that a true understanding of consciousness will require laws as yet unknown.

Alfred Rupert Sheldrake is an English author and parapsychology researcher. He proposed the concept of morphic resonance, a conjecture that lacks mainstream acceptance and has been widely criticized as pseudoscience.

items.title: Raymond Tallis - The Search for Meaning
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

To search for meaning, God or something like God is often involved. But this need not be so. How can meaning be found, or purpose realized, without invoking supernatural forces? Scientists and philosophers who do not believe in God search for ‘meaning’ in what seems to be a meaningless cosmos. They do generate a kind of ‘meaning’. Are they fooling themselves?

Raymond C. Tallis is a philosopher, poet, novelist, cultural critic and a retired medical physician and clinical neuroscientist.

Sexual questions abound. What is “normal” sexual behavior vs. multiple variations? How to appreciate LGBTQ+ issues? What can be learned from sex in animal models that is relevant for human sexuality? A philosophical perspective can provide clarity.

items.title: William Dembski - Fine-Tuning in the Universe
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Sign up for a Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

We human beings sit roughly midway between the sizes of atoms and galaxies, and both must be so perfectly structured for us to exist. It’s called ‘fine-tuning’ and it’s all so breathtakingly precise that it cries out for explanation. To some, fine-tuning leads to God. To others, there are non-supernatural explanations. Both are startling.

Support the show with a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring life's deepest questions: shorturl.at/OnyRq

William Albert Dembski is an American mathematician, philosopher, and theologian. He was a proponent of intelligent design pseudoscience, specifically the concept of specified complexity, and was a senior fellow of the Discovery Institute's Center for Science and Culture.

items.title: Eric Schwitzgebel - What is Belief?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Listen to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts. Subscribe today: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Everyone has beliefs—some are simple and basic (e.g., my name, age), others complex and controversial (e.g., God? Soul? Politics? Morality?). But what is the concept of ‘belief’? What does it take for some statement to be a ‘belief’? And what distinguishes among ‘belief’, ‘true belief’, and ‘knowledge’? Before asking what we believe, let’s understand ‘belief’.

Make a tax-deductible donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Eric Schwitzgebel is an American professor of philosophy at the University of California, Riverside. His main interests include connections between empirical psychology, philosophy of mind, and the nature of belief, as well as Classical Chinese philosophy.

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Follow us on Instagram for daily news, announcements, and exciting updates: https://shorturl.at/p2IhM

The fine-tuning of the constants of nature, which seems required for the existence of stars and planets and certainly for life and mind, is a fascinating feature of our universe. But before grand metaphysical schemes are advanced by philosophers, theologians, and even scientists, proper understanding of the underlying assumptions and fundamental physics are needed.

Bernard J. Carr is a British professor of mathematics and astronomy at Queen Mary University of London. His research interests include the early universe, dark matter, general relativity, primordial black holes, and the anthropic principle.

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Shop hoodies, mugs, notebooks, and more in the Closer To Truth merch store: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Enlightenment is the claimed higher mental states that characterize various Eastern religious and philosophical traditions. Such enlightenment is usually achieved through kinds of meditative practices. While there is no doubt that deep and protracted mediation can alter normal conscious states, is there any real reach to ultimate reality?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Jeffery Martin is a social scientist and the current director of the Center for the Study of Non-Symbolic Consciousness. He specializes in bringing rigorous empirical research and testing to transformational techniques and theories that have previously been supported anecdotally. Martin is a leading expert on persistent non-symbolic consciousness (enlightenment, nonduality, mystical experience, union with God/nature, etc.).

items.title: Alan Torrance - Do Major Religions Worship the Same God?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Register for a Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

The many different religions of the world proclaim radically different, often opposing and contradictory doctrines about God. How then can their believers be worshipping the same God? Can the very general, soft areas of agreement compensate for the very specific, hard tenets of disagreement?

Alan Torrance is professor of systematic theology at St Mary's College of the University of St. Andrews. Previously he lectured at King's College London where he was also Director of the Research Institute in Systematic Theology. During this time he served as Senior Research Fellow at the Erasmus Institute, University of Notre Dame. He previously lectured at Knox Theological Hall and the University of Otago, Dunedin, New Zealand.

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Follow us on X for daily articles, updates, and more: https://x.com/CloserToTruth

God exists? God does not exist? What kinds of Gods? It’s no challenge to find flaws and fallacies on all sides. Can we step away from old arguments and ask how to approach God’s existence? What’s the process? What’s the way of thinking? What are issues, problems, questions about God and God’s existence? Can conventional wisdom be avoided? Can progress be made?

Register for a Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

William Albert Dembski is an American mathematician, philosopher and theologian. He was a proponent of intelligent design pseudoscience, specifically the concept of specified complexity, and was a senior fellow of the Discovery Institute's Center for Science and Culture.

items.title: Bahador Bahrami - How Art Affects the Self
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

There is increasing evidence in the field of neuroscience that art enhances brain function by impacting brain wave patterns, emotions, and the nervous system. But more abstractly, does art have the power to affect the self? That is, a person’s beliefs, thoughts, feelings, identity. What impact does art have on the individual consuming it?

Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Bahador Bahrami is a Senior Scientist and Director of Crowd Cognition Lab in Department of Psychology at Ludwig Maximilian University in Munich, Germany and Professor of Social Neuroscience at Department of Psychology at Royal Holloway University of London.

items.title: Philip Kitcher - Philosophy of Biological Ethics & New Norms
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Support Closer To Truth with a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help us continue exploring in the new year: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Can biology—especially evolutionary biology—help explain ethics, and if so, can science influence ethics and help set new norms? If science can, should science do? Topics range from issues of sex/gender to punishment for crime.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Philip Stuart Kitcher is a British philosopher who is the John Dewey Professor Emeritus of philosophy at Columbia University. He specializes in the philosophy of science, the philosophy of biology, the philosophy of mathematics, the philosophy of literature, and more recently pragmatism.

items.title: Hugh McCann - Is the Future Open?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Register for a Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

Why is an ‘open’ future controversial among theologians? If the future is not ‘settled’, if there is no certainty from God’s viewpoint of how future events will turn out, then God’s providence, could not be as sure. If the future is truly open, then not even God could know the future because there are no future facts to know.

items.title: William Lane Craig - Does God Know Everything?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Get special exclusives and discounts with a free Closer To Truth membership today: https://closertotruth.com/.

What is the difference between God knowing an infinite amount of things and God knowing literally everything? ‘Everything’ is vastly larger than infinity, if that makes sense—everything includes infinities of infinities. Moreover, how could God, if God exists, know everything? By what ineffable process could such unimaginable knowledge be obtained?

Support Closer To Truth with a tax-deductible donation of any amount: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

William Lane Craig is an American analytic philosopher, Christian apologist, author, and Wesleyan theologian who upholds the view of Molinism and neo-Apollinarianism. He is a professor of philosophy at Houston Christian University and at the Talbot School of Theology of Biola University.

items.title: James Tabor - What is Immortality?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>This holiday, support Closer To Truth with a tax-deductible donation of any amount: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

What would it be like to live forever? Immortality is the promise of most religions, thwarting the looming fateful finality of death. Everything in science screams ‘Impossible!’ Even the universe will not last forever. How then to understand immortality? Are the only arguments philosophical as well as theological? Could there be a chance?

Register for a free Closer To Truth account to get special exclusives and discounts at https://closertotruth.com/.

James Daniel Tabor is an American Biblical scholar and retired Professor of Ancient Judaism and Early Christianity in the Department of Religious Studies at the University of North Carolina at Charlotte, where he taught from 1989 until 2022 and served as chair from 2004 to 2014.

items.title: N.T. Wright - What is God's Judgment?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Love the show? Make a donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Every religion thunders its day of reckoning. To examine God’s judgment is to probe God’s character and perhaps assess God’s existence. If God does not exist, God’s judgment may expose it. If God does exist, and assuming God is merciful as well as just, maybe we’ve missed the meaning of God’s judgment.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Nicholas Thomas Wright FRSE, known as N. T. Wright or Tom Wright, is an English New Testament scholar, Pauline theologian and Anglican bishop. He was the bishop of Durham from 2003 to 2010.

items.title: Alvin Plantinga - Is This the Best of All Possible Worlds?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Shop our merch and get a unique holiday gift for anyone on your list: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Is this a joke? Our world, with its vast evils, the ‘best possible world’? It seems absurd. Yet some do claim that ultimately our world will be the best possible world, because the sum total of all the good, primarily generated by human free will and divine providence, will overpower all the obvious evils. Perhaps the calculus should include the entire cosmos?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Alvin Carl Plantinga is an American analytic philosopher who works primarily in the fields of philosophy of religion, epistemology, and logic. From 1963 to 1982, Plantinga taught at Calvin University before accepting an appointment as the John A. O'Brien Professor of Philosophy at the University of Notre Dame.

Closer To Truth host Robert Lawrence Kuhn recently published an extensive new article titled "A Landscape of Consciousness: Toward a taxonomy of explanations and implications". Robert Lawrence Kuhn, PhD., is the co-creator, executive producer, writer, and host of Closer To Truth, the PBS/public television series on Cosmos, Life, Mind, and Meaning that presents leading scientists, philosophers, and creative thinkers discussing the fundamental questions of existence. items.title: Brian Leftow - Is Eternal Life Possible?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<html><head></head><body>Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for news, announcements, and exciting updates: https://shorturl.at/p2IhM

How to imagine the experience of eternal life? Would we sense ourselves? How would we feel? Whom would we know? What would we do? What would God do? Living forever seems so absurd, yet eternal life is the promise of almost every religion. But if we cannot even imagine what eternal life will be, how can we have hope in its reality?

Donate to Closer To Truth this holiday season and help us keep our content free and without paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Brian Leftow is a philosopher specializing in philosophy of religion, medieval philosophy, and metaphysics. He is the William P. Alston Professor for the Philosophy of Religion at Rutgers University.
